<project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0"
         xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"

    <artifactId>ingestion</artifactId>
    <name>ingestion</name>
    <description>ingestion</description>

    <dependencies>
        <!-- 1 Normal business logic dependency: only depends on the main code (src/main/java) of the common module, excluding any test code. -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.fortinet.nac</groupId>
            <artifactId>common</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.fortinet.nac</groupId>
            <artifactId>classification</artifactId>
            <!-- Explicit version is required for classifier-based dependency resolution. -->
            <version>${project.version}</version>
            <!-- Depend on the attached plain library artifact, not the repackaged Boot main artifact. -->
            <classifier>plain</classifier>
            <exclusions>
                <exclusion>
                    <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
                    <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-web</artifactId>
                </exclusion>
            </exclusions>
        </dependency>

        <!-- Redisson dependency for direct Redis operations -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.redisson</groupId>
            <artifactId>redisson</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <!-- 2 Used to depend on the test code of the common module -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.fortinet.nac</groupId>
            <artifactId>common</artifactId>
            <version>${project.version}</version>
            <type>test-jar</type>
            <scope>test</scope>
        </dependency>

        <!-- The following 3 dependencies are for Redis/MariaDB testing against docker container in non-production environments -->
        <!-- Core Testcontainers -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.testcontainers</groupId>
            <artifactId>testcontainers</artifactId>
            <scope>test</scope>
        </dependency>
        <!-- Testcontainers + JUnit5 -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.testcontainers</groupId>
            <artifactId>junit-jupiter</artifactId>
            <scope>test</scope>
        </dependency>
        <!-- MariaDB Container -->
         <dependency>

            <artifactId>mariadb</artifactId>
            <scope>test</scope>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-data-jpa</artifactId>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.mariadb.jdbc</groupId>
            <artifactId>mariadb-java-client</artifactId>
            <scope>runtime</scope>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-validation</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-devtools</artifactId>
            <!-- runtime: Not needed during compile time, but used during runtime, and not packaged in the production JAR -->
            <!-- Compile time: Your source code does not directly import classes from devtools, so it's not needed during compilation. -->
            <!-- Runtime: Spring Boot automatically detects and loads hot reload features from devtools during startup. -->
            <!-- runtime tells Maven:
                 "I don’t need to include its classes at compile time,
                  but I want it on the classpath at runtime." -->
            <scope>runtime</scope>
            <!-- optional: true means that if module A depends on current module, A won't inherit devtools; A must declare it explicitly to use. -->
            <optional>true</optional>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.projectlombok</groupId>
            <artifactId>lombok</artifactId>
            <optional>true</optional>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-test</artifactId>
            <!-- scope: test means it's only effective during testing (e.g., mvn test).
                 It will not be included in the final packaged JAR. -->
            <scope>test</scope>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-webflux</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>commons-codec</groupId>
            <artifactId>commons-codec</artifactId>
        </dependency>

    </dependencies>

    <build>
        <finalName>ingestion</finalName>

        <plugins>
            <!-- Responsible for compiling Java source code -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-compiler-plugin</artifactId>

            <!-- Package the project into a JAR (with a main function); allows running the app via mvn spring-boot:run -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
                <artifactId>spring-boot-maven-plugin</artifactId>
            </plugin>
        </plugins>
    </build>
</project>

file.content:
server:
  port: 8086

spring:
  application:
    name: ingestion

  redis:
    # host and port will be overridden by Testcontainers automatically
    host: localhost
    port: 6379
    timeout: 15000

    # Redisson-specific configuration
    redisson:
      # Connection pool settings
      #
      # Maximum number of connections in Redis command connection pool.
      # Redisson doesn't distinguish max-idle, only cares about: maximum pool size and minimum keep-alive connections.
      #
      # Connection pool optimized for 24 stream consumers + business requests
      connection-pool-size: 96

      # Minimum number of idle connections to retain in the pool.
      # Even if Redis isn't accessed for some time, keep at least this many connections ready.
      # Helps avoid "cold start latency" — no need to establish a new connection when requests resume.
      #
      # High QPS scenario, pre-warm 24 connections.
      # Keep a batch of connections resident in the pool, aligned with consumer count.
      connection-minimum-idle-size: 24

      # Lettuce shares Pub/Sub and regular connections in the same pool (or a few independent).
      # But Redisson is different.
      #
      # Project currently doesn't use Pub/Sub.
      subscription-connection-pool-size: 8
      subscription-connection-minimum-idle-size: 2

      # Redisson thread model:
      #      nettyThreads:	IO threads
      #      threads:       	Redisson workers (locks/callbacks/scheduling)
      # These two thread pools are global, not per-bean
      #
      # Recommended empirical values:
      #      nettyThreads ≈ CPU * 2
      #      threads ≈ CPU
      netty-threads: 16
      threads: 8

      # Retry settings
      retry-attempts: 5
      retry-interval: 1000

  datasource:
    url: jdbc:mariadb://localhost/bsc?localSocket=/var/lib/mysql/mysql.sock&zeroDateTimeBehavior=convertToNull&useUnicode=true&characterEncoding=UTF-8&CharSet=UTF-8
    username: root
    password: ""
    driver-class-name: org.mariadb.jdbc.Driver

  jpa:
    hibernate:
      ddl-auto: validate
      naming:
        physical-strategy: org.hibernate.boot.model.naming.PhysicalNamingStrategyStandardImpl
    show-sql: false
    database-platform: org.hibernate.dialect.MariaDBDialect

logging:
  config: classpath:logback-spring.xml



# =====================================================
# NAC Stream consumer & producer configuration (used by RedisStreamConsumerBase)
# =====================================================
nac:
  stream:
    max-length: 200000            # Maximum number of messages to retain in stream
    trim-enabled: true
    trim-every-n-writes: 1000

    internal:
      poll-timeout-ms: 1000             # to avoid busy poll
      message-batch-size-per-poll: 20   # Too large batches will increase p95/max (worse tail latency).
      redis-consumer-count: 16
      worker-thread-pool-size: 32
    external:
      poll-timeout-ms: 1000             # to avoid busy poll
      message-batch-size-per-poll: 50
      redis-consumer-count: 8
      worker-thread-pool-size: 8

    consume-max-retry: 3              # Max retry attempts on message consumption failure
    create-group-include-history: true  # Include historical messages when creating group during tests
    pending:
      idle-threshold-ms: 10000        # Consider stuck if not ACKed within 10 seconds
      scan-interval-ms: 5000          # Scan pending messages every 5 seconds
      claim-batch-size: 10            # Number of messages to claim per XPENDING batch


# =====================================================
# Ingestion Controller Reactive Netty Config
# =====================================================
ingestion:
    recovery:
      enabled: true
      stale-threshold-seconds: 80
      fixed-delay-ms: 30000
      batch-size: 2000
      rate-limit-per-second: 100
      async:
        core-size: 4
        max-size: 16
        queue-capacity: 100
        keep-alive-seconds: 60
    reactive:
      thread-pool:
        core-size: 30
        max-size: 300
        queue-capacity: 10000
        keep-alive-seconds: 60

# =====================================================
# Metrics
# =====================================================
management:
  endpoints:
    web:
      exposure:
        # /metrics is for: JVM / thread pools / custom metrics
        include: health,info,prometheus,metrics
  metrics:
    distribution:
      # Only enable histogram for lock-hold metric (not global),
      # so we can compute p95/p99 from *_bucket while keeping overhead low.
      percentiles-histogram:
        policy_reproc_lock_hold_seconds: true
      # Histogram bucket lower bound for fine-grained range.
      # Values below 1ms are still counted (not dropped), but won't be over-segmented.
      minimum-expected-value:
        policy_reproc_lock_hold_seconds: 1ms
      # Histogram bucket upper bound for fine-grained range.
      # Values above 60s are still counted (not dropped), and fall into top bucket(s)
      # instead of creating more detailed buckets at very large durations.
      maximum-expected-value:
        policy_reproc_lock_hold_seconds: 60s

# =====================================================
# Policy Reprocess Job Configuration
# =====================================================
policy:
  reprocess:
    # 1. producer side
    mark-init-batch-size: 1000

    # 2. cron job side
    scan-interval-ms: 30000
    scan-batch-size: 200
    max-candidates-per-scan: 2000
    scan-lock-ttl-seconds: 900
    rate-limit-per-second: 100

    # listener thread pool: handles PolicyReprocessRequestedEvent trigger
    listener:
      core-size: 1
      max-size: 2
      queue-capacity: 100
      keep-alive-seconds: 60

    # job thread pool: executes per-entity reprocess tasks inside each scan batch
    job:
      core-size: 8
      max-size: 16
      queue-capacity: 32
      keep-alive-seconds: 60

        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.mariadb.jdbc</groupId>
            <artifactId>mariadb-java-client</artifactId>
            <scope>runtime</scope>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-validation</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-devtools</artifactId>
            <!-- runtime: Not needed during compile time, but used during runtime, and not packaged in the production JAR -->
            <!-- Compile time: Your source code does not directly import classes from devtools, so it's not needed during compilation. -->
            <!-- Runtime: Spring Boot automatically detects and loads hot reload features from devtools during startup. -->
            <!-- runtime tells Maven:
                 "I don’t need to include its classes at compile time,
                  but I want it on the classpath at runtime." -->
            <scope>runtime</scope>
            <!-- optional: true means that if module A depends on current module, A won't inherit devtools; A must declare it explicitly to use. -->
            <optional>true</optional>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.projectlombok</groupId>
            <artifactId>lombok</artifactId>
            <optional>true</optional>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-test</artifactId>
            <!-- scope: test means it's only effective during testing (e.g., mvn test).
                 It will not be included in the final packaged JAR. -->
            <scope>test</scope>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-webflux</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>commons-codec</groupId>
            <artifactId>commons-codec</artifactId>
        </dependency>

    </dependencies>

    <build>
        <finalName>ingestion</finalName>

        <plugins>
            <!-- Responsible for compiling Java source code -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-compiler-plugin</artifactId>
            </plugin>

            <!-- Package the project into a JAR (with a main function); allows running the app via mvn spring-boot:run -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
                <artifactId>spring-boot-maven-plugin</artifactId>
            </plugin>
        </plugins>
    </build>
</project>
